item.title: A French jeweller once traded a $1 million necklace and $100 to buy a Fifth Avenue mansion in 1917
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Deadly landslide strikes China, dozens remain missing
item.dc:creator: Prisha Patnaik

item.title: Canadian gym tells Sikh TikToker to leave over carrying kirpan
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: H-1B update: No second lottery, USCIS says it has received enough petitions for FY 2027
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists played human voices near Alaska salmon streams and found that bears and eagles fled
item.description: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Chinese CEO Yang Zhilin achieves huge AI milestone, Indian-origin entrepreneur says US was stupid enough to let him go
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Rare Atlantic Niña and Super El Niño may suppress hurricanes, lowering US landfall risk
item.description: A rare Atlantic Niña is developing alongside a strong Super El Niño event. These ocean patterns may suppress hurricane formation in the Atlantic Ocean. Increased wind shear and sinking air create an atmospheric shield against storms. However, experts caution that a quieter season does not eliminate hurricane risks. Residents in vulnerable areas should continue their normal hurricane preparations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists discover first-ever antidote to red tide toxin
item.description: In a groundbreaking study, researchers have identified a frog protein capable of neutralizing saxitoxin, a harmful neurotoxin. This unique protein functions as a molecular sponge, effectively binding to the toxin in the bloodstream. Experiments on mice demonstrated its ability to prevent and even reverse the effects of lethal poisoning, offering significant possibilities for combatting paralytic shellfish poisoning incidents globally.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 'Absurd': Putin critic Boris Nadezhdin found guilty in 'extremist symbols' case
item.description: Russian opposition politician Boris Nadezhdin was convicted of displaying extremist symbols. This ruling prevents him from contesting upcoming parliamentary elections in Russia. Nadezhdin had previously criticized President Vladimir Putin and the Ukraine war. Another Putin critic, Ilya Remeslo, was also arrested on charges of spreading false military information. Russian authorities have intensified crackdowns on dissent and free speech since 2022.
item.dc:creator: Karan Manral

item.title: Who is Swaran Singh? Indian-origin psychiatrist to join House of Lords as UK announces new peerages
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This billionaire’s $300 million superyacht cruised through the Strait of Hormuz without any problems
item.description: Motor Yacht A, under the ownership of billionaire Andrey Melnichenko, recently traversed the Strait of Hormuz amidst increased military tensions that have prompted commercial vessels to postpone their voyages. Following the imposition of Western sanctions after Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Melnichenko relocated his assets to the Gulf, particularly after his Sailing Yacht A was confiscated by Italian authorities in March 2022.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Who is D nexT': Iran billboard targets Trump after Graham's death
item.dc:creator: Prisha Patnaik

item.title: China’s giant sinkholes are protecting an endangered tree, but they may also be trapping its future
item.description: In China, vast sinkholes provide a refuge for the rare Magnolia aromatica trees, buffering them against the harsh realities of climate change. These subterranean havens create stable microclimates that support these endangered species. However, this isolation can limit genetic diversity and hinder the trees' adaptability to future environmental changes. To ensure their survival, conservation efforts must focus on safeguarding habitats while facilitating gene flow between populations.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: It's over for international researchers in the US: Experts ask how PhD students will finish their course in 4 years
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: In 1980, Mount St Helens buried a river valley in volcanic sediment. Then 58 beavers helped turn the wasteland into a thriving wetland
item.description: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistan's fifth province? Gilgit-Baltistan assembly passes key resolution amid PoK unrest
item.dc:creator: Abhishek Mishra

item.title: 7 Americans move into Kenya isolation centre as US mandates 21-day Ebola quarantine
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Pelé: "Success isn't determined by how many times you win, but..."
item.description: Pelé believed true success is revealed after a loss, not solely by victories. How individuals handle doubt after defeat shows their long-term durability. A win confirms preparation worked, while a loss creates doubt to overcome. Returning to routine after setbacks creates opportunities to move forward. This principle applies beyond sports to various life challenges and failures.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Don't use China as an issue': Beijing hits back after Trump alleges election 'meddling'
item.description: China asked Donald Trump to stop using the nation as an election issue. Trump accused Beijing of interfering in the 2020 US presidential election. He also claimed China illicitly acquired millions of American voter files. China's embassy rejected these allegations, calling US elections an internal matter. Beijing urged the US to foster better bilateral relations.
item.dc:creator: Karan Manral

item.title: Scientists studied 30 Sierra Nevada ponds that can swing 20°C in a day. Shrinking snowpack may make them smaller, warmer and more nutrient-rich
item.description: Mountain ponds in California's Sierra Nevada show significant daily temperature changes. Winter snowpack profoundly influences pond size and water temperature throughout summer. Less snowfall leads to warmer, smaller ponds with concentrated nutrients. Climate change projections indicate reduced snowpack, impacting these delicate ecosystems. These small water bodies play a crucial role in mountain biodiversity and nutrient cycling.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
